QF.id: Evaluating Fund Performance and Fund Selection_37

question.text: Fund P returned 20% with SD of 16%. Fund Q returned 15% with SD of 6%. Risk-free rate is 5%. Which fund has a superior Sharpe Ratio?

answer.text: Fund P with Sharpe Ratio of 1.25
Fund Q with Sharpe Ratio of 1.67
Fund P because it has higher returns

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Fund P Sharpe = (20%-5%)/16% = 15%/16% = 0.9375. Fund Q Sharpe = (15%-5%)/6% = 10%/6% = 1.667. Fund Q has a significantly better risk-adjusted return. **The Trap:** Candidates may pick Fund P because it has higher absolute returns (20% vs 15%), ignoring that Fund Q achieved its returns with far less volatility. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is a powerful example of why risk-adjusted metrics matter. Higher returns do not automatically mean better performance if achieved with disproportionate risk. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Fund Q is the better choice for most clients. Only recommend Fund P to highly aggressive investors who can stomach a standard deviation of 16%.
